acceptance_targets: [AWARD_SCHEMATIC_DOUBLE_SETPIECE] At least one half of the revised chapter departs materially from the current call-room or block setup to split-by-split race narration to result-board sequence, and the Lena material buys a concrete new complication beyond proving that the patch makes her slower. If both halves still run the same scaffold with different event names, this finding fails.

[CRAFT_OVERSTATEMENT_AFTER_LANDING] After Lena touches the pad and after Kai crosses the finish, every remaining paragraph must either add a concrete new pressure on-page or materially alter public or relational state. If a paragraph can be removed from those aftermath spans without losing any event, witness, or consequence because it only restates what the race already proved, this finding fails.

[PROSE_ELLIPTICAL_CLIPPING] In the revised race passages anchored to those lines, the pacing remains fast but the narration no longer uses standalone clipped markers such as "The wall." or "A hundred and fifty." as structural beats. A reader should be able to move through both races without encountering repeated subjectless or article-stripped fragments that carry the action by themselves.
[PROSE_WRIST_ACHE_chapter_17] Ch 17 contains exactly two wrist references. Neither restates healing origin. The mid-race reference (line 3661) retains its tethering function.

[READER_RETEACH_chapter_17] Lena's race in Ch 17 contains no more than one before/after comparison with the pre-patch system. The rest is experienced in real-time sensation. Kai's call room section does not re-explain his patch status.
[PROSE_FLAT_DEFAULT_chapter_17] Line 3639 describes the stadium light without 'flat.' The light quality distinguishes the Doha venue from the Kirchberg facility.
[R002] Ch 17 does not enumerate spinal bridge, bilateral leg grafts, and visual processing as a list; wrist description is one sentence or fewer.
[R009] Ch 17 does not re-explain what the partial patch does or describe the ambient optimization layer being silent; the post-patch state is referenced in one sentence or fewer.
[R018] Ch 17 contains no more than 7 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 92 prose lines).
[R043] Ch 17 has no more than 24 sentences (≤7% of ~344) beginning with 'She.'

findings.acceptance_test: At least one half of the revised chapter departs materially from the current call-room or block setup to split-by-split race narration to result-board sequence, and the Lena material buys a concrete new complication beyond proving that the patch makes her slower. If both halves still run the same scaffold with different event names, this finding fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's two-race architecture feels engineered rather than singular. Lena's half spends substantial page space re-proving a cost the book already established, then Kai's half arrives on a very similar call-room-to-race-to-result scaffold at higher intensity. The result is thematic symmetry without enough formal differentiation: both sequences read as athlete enters arena, narrates incremental splits, then privately revalues a public loss.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the halves at the structural level in the Lena build and aftermath around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:3; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:65, or compress that material so the chapter is not spending comparable real estate on a smaller dramatic payload before the Kai set piece at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:73; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:113; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:167. Give Lena either a sharper public complication beyond the podium result or a leaner build so the two halves are doing genuinely different work.

findings.acceptance_test: After Lena touches the pad and after Kai crosses the finish, every remaining paragraph must either add a concrete new pressure on-page or materially alter public or relational state. If a paragraph can be removed from those aftermath spans without losing any event, witness, or consequence because it only restates what the race already proved, this finding fails.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps paraphrasing meanings the action has already made legible. Lena's bodily ownership is clear before the scoreboard, but the narration then translates the result into thesis language; Kai's override is fully legible when he arrests the sprint and walks the last thirty meters, yet the closing paragraphs restate that the crowd misunderstood and that his legs are his. The aftermath buys emphasis more than new consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the post-arrival passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:53; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:67 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:161; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:175; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:181 so that material after the physical landing either introduces a new public consequence, witness, or decision, or gets cut. Preserve the must-land beats of Lena finishing third on her own terms and Kai overriding the system to finish under his own power, but stop re-proving those beats in abstract summary.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised race passages anchored to those lines, the pacing remains fast but the narration no longer uses standalone clipped markers such as "The wall." or "A hundred and fifty." as structural beats. A reader should be able to move through both races without encountering repeated subjectless or article-stripped fragments that carry the action by themselves.
findings.problem: The race passages rely on clipped beat-markers and sentence fragments often enough to become a governing chapter rhythm: "The starting tone.", "First fifty.", "The wall.", "Not open air.", "A hundred and fifty.", "His vision cracked." The constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration, and here the telegraphic scaffolding makes the prose feel mechanically segmented rather than pressure-compressed and embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped race scaffolds in the Lena sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:23; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:29; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:35; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:45 and the Kai sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:125; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129 into grammatically complete, still-fast sentences. Keep compression and acceleration, but do it through syntax and sensory selection rather than noun-fragment drumbeats.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17 contains exactly two wrist references. Neither restates healing origin. The mid-race reference (line 3661) retains its tethering function.

evidence.excerpt: 3617: It did this. The persistent, dull complaint of bone that had been knitted too fast by synthetic grafts five months ago — the break from the wall at the curve of the indoor track at Kirchberg, the moment the system drove his legs at full sprint speed while his mind was in a void and his body met concrete at thirty-seven kilometers per hour. The wrist had healed in nine days. It had never stopped telling him that nine days was wrong.

3635: Kai stood. His wrist ached. His left cheek, where the lacerations from the crash had scarred into faint silver lines that the broadcast makeup team had offered to conceal, pulled slightly when he set his jaw. He'd told them no. The scars were from the last time his body left without him. He wanted to feel them when he ran.

3661: A hundred meters. The straight. The field strung out — Okafor in lane four had the lead, his stride absurdly long, the turnover rate so fast it looked mechanical because it partly was. Kai was fourth. Maybe fifth. The wrist ached with each arm pump, a tiny flare of wrong that tethered him to his own skeleton.

3721: Kai stood on the track. His wrist ached. His vision was clear. His legs were shaking, the synthetic and biological components settling back into their uneasy coexistence now that the crisis had passed, the bridge going quiet at the base of his spine.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 3617: It did this. The persistent, dull complaint of bone that had been knitted too fast by synthetic grafts five months ago — the break from the wall at the curve of the indoor track at Kirchberg, the moment the system drove his legs at full sprint speed while his mind was in a void and his body met concrete at thirty-seven kilometers per hour. The wrist had healed in nine days. It had never stopped telling him that nine days was wrong.

findings.problem: Four wrist references in one chapter, including the longest backstory restatement in the book (line 3617: full sentence recapping the break, the wall, the sprint speed, the healing timeline). Lines 3635 and 3721 add 'His wrist ached' with no new information.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3617, cut the full-sentence backstory entirely. By Ch 17 the reader needs no reminder. Keep two wrist references total: one in the call room (line 3619 thumb-pressing gesture, which is active and scene-specific) and one mid-race (line 3661 'a tiny flare of wrong that tethered him to his own skeleton' — the tethering metaphor is new and dramatically functional). Cut lines 3635 and 3721.

findings.acceptance_test: Lena's race in Ch 17 contains no more than one before/after comparison with the pre-patch system. The rest is experienced in real-time sensation. Kai's call room section does not re-explain his patch status.

evidence.excerpt: 3549: All of them on Meridian's patch. All of them running integrated systems that smoothed the space between intent and action until there was no space at all — thought becoming motion, seamless, optimized, the body a tool that never surprised the person using it.

3567: She hit the water. The shock of it — not the temperature, which was regulated to twenty-seven degrees, but the texture. Ordinary water. It had weight and resistance and it did not resolve into the granular sensory language she had lived inside for twenty-two years. Before the patch, the water had spoken to her — every eddy and pressure gradient a signal, the drag against her skin a continuous data stream her system translated into micro-adjustments of hand angle, kick depth, body rotation. Now it was water. Wet, dense, chlorinated, cold where the circulation pushed a current sideways across her lane.

3575: Her lungs were working. She noticed this the way she'd been noticing it for weeks now — not the managed version the system used to run, distributing oxygen debt across muscle groups to delay the buildup of lactate, smoothing the burn until it was more signal than suffering. Just her lungs. Filling and emptying on a rhythm her body chose. She was breathing harder than she would have been at this pace six months ago. Effort felt like effort now. That was the patch's cost, or its gift, or both at once.

3623: Kai wasn't running any rehearsal. His system was what Meridian had installed three years ago: the spinal neural bridge, the bilateral synthetic muscle grafts in both legs, the visual processing enhancement. Unpatched. No Meridian firmware update. No Yusuf's partial patch. Nothing between him and the virus except the original hardware and whatever he'd taught himself in the months since his body ran him into a wall.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
3549: All of them on Meridian's patch. All of them running integrated systems that smoothed the space between intent and action until there was no space at all — thought becoming motion, seamless, optimized, the body a tool that never surprised the person using it.
3550: 
3551: Lena touched the port at the base of her skull. A habit she'd had since childhood. The hardware was still there. Yusuf's patch hadn't removed it — it had just quieted the conversation. The system still ran, still processed sensory input and motor commands at some reduced level, but the ambient optimization layer that had been adjusting her stroke pattern, managing her fatigue distribution, smoothing her kick timing since she was four years old — that was silent. Had been silent for weeks now. She was still getting used to the quiet. Some mornings she woke and reached for the background hum of system activity the way you'd reach for a phone on a nightstand, and the absence startled her all over again.
3552: 
3553: "Morozova. Lane five."
3554: 
[...]
3570: 
3571: First fifty. She felt the wall coming, loaded her turn, flipped. The rotation was hers — a turn she'd practiced ten thousand times, the muscle memory...

findings.problem: During Lena's race, the narration repeatedly re-explains what the system used to do vs. what it does now: 'the ambient optimization layer that had been adjusting her stroke pattern... since she was four years old' (3551), 'Not the managed version the system used to run' (3575). During Kai's call room, line 3623 re-explains his unpatched status. The race scenes should be experienced, not explained.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3549-3575, reduce the before/after comparisons. The reader knows what Lena lost. During the race, let the absence speak through sensation: her arms getting tired, her catch getting sloppy, the field pulling away. One brief acknowledgment of the silence is enough ('Her lungs were working — just her lungs' or similar). At line 3623, cut the explanation of Kai's unpatched status; the reader knows.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 3639 describes the stadium light without 'flat.' The light quality distinguishes the Doha venue from the Kirchberg facility.

evidence.excerpt: 3639: Not open air. The stadium had a retractable roof, closed against the Doha heat, and the air conditioning turned the enclosed space into something cool and strange — forty-two degrees pressing against glass above, twenty-three degrees on the track surface below. The light was flat and white, broadcast-quality, leaving no shadow on the synthetic rubber surface. Forty thousand people in tiered seats that rose up into the upper reaches of the stadium where the glass roof met steel. He could feel the crowd in his sternum.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 3639: Not open air. The stadium had a retractable roof, closed against the Doha heat, and the air conditioning turned the enclosed space into something cool and strange — forty-two degrees pressing against glass above, twenty-three degrees on the track surface below. The light was flat and white, broadcast-quality, leaving no shadow on the synthetic rubber surface. Forty thousand people in tiered seats that rose up into the upper reaches of the stadium where the glass roof met steel. He could feel the crowd in his sternum.

findings.problem: 'The light was flat and white, broadcast-quality' — the stadium light is described with the same 'flat white' construction used for fluorescent lighting throughout the facility chapters. The Doha stadium should have its own light signature distinct from Kirchberg's fluorescents.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3639, describe the broadcast lighting through its specific qualities: the shadowlessness, the heat of it, the way it makes skin look. The point is that this light is different from Kirchberg — it should be described differently.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17 does not re-explain what the partial patch does or describe the ambient optimization layer being silent; the post-patch state is referenced in one sentence or fewer.
findings.evidence: Ch 17 line 3551: 'the ambient optimization layer that had been adjusting her stroke pattern, managing her fatigue distribution, smoothing her kick timing since she was four years old — that was silent.'; Ch 16 line 3332: 'The silence in her head where the system's ambient processing had been was becoming, if not comfortable, then at least recognizable.'
findings.problem: Lena's post-patch silence is described in adjacent chapters (Ch 16 and Ch 17) using the same framing: the system's ambient processing is now silent, she is adjusting to quiet. Ch 16 establishes this; Ch 17 re-explains at greater length.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17 contains no more than 7 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 92 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 17: 46/92 prose lines contain em-dashes = 50.0%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 17 has em-dash density of 50.0%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 17 from 46 to ~7 (39 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17 has no more than 24 sentences (≤7% of ~344) beginning with 'She.'
findings.problem: Ch 17 has 'She'-opener density of 9.6%, well above the 7% threshold. This creates a rhythmic monotony where consecutive sentences beginning with 'She' flatten the prose into a subject-verb march.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 17 from 33 to ~24 (9 rewrites). Vary sentence openings: lead with setting, sensory detail, dependent clauses, object-first constructions, or dialogue attributions.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
